UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents a user of the LibTrack Analytics application, including both administrative staff and visitors.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity.

email.description: The institutional email address of the user. Used for login and identification.

fullName.description: The full name of the user.

role.description: The role of the user within the application. 'admin' for administrators, 'visitor' for general users.

blocked.description: Indicates if the user is currently blocked from accessing the system. (Default: false)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was last updated.

Visit.title: Visit
Visit.description: Represents a single visit event by a user to either the library or the Dean's office, capturing relevant details and timestamps.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Visit entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile entity who made this visit. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Visit)

checkInTime.description: Timestamp when the visitor checked in.

checkOutTime.description: Optional timestamp when the visitor checked out or left the premises. More relevant for Dean's office visits but can be used for duration tracking in the library.

collegeDepartment.description: The college department the visitor belongs to. Primarily for library visits and filtering usage statistics.

reason.description: The primary reason or purpose for the visit. Can be a free-text entry or selected from predefined options, serving both 'Reason for Visit' (Library) and 'Purpose of Visit' (Dean's Office) requirements.

studentEmployeeId.description: The student or employee ID of the visitor, typically used for Dean's office visits as an additional identifier.

status.description: The current status of the visit, primarily for Dean's office appointments to track progress. Possible values: 'waiting', 'in-meeting', 'completed'.

definition.description: Stores individual user profiles. The document ID `{userId}` matches the Firebase Auth UID. Includes denormalized 'role' field for UI logic and a 'blocked' field for user management. This is the primary source of user data for the application.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, matching their Firebase Authentication UID.

definition.description: Records individual visits by users to the library or Dean's office. Includes denormalized 'userId' for authorization independence, allowing security rules to directly verify ownership without 'get()' operations on parent documents. Also contains 'collegeDepartment', 'reason', and 'status' for statistics and appointment tracking.

params.name: visitId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific visit event.

definition.description: A dedicated collection for robust administrative role assignment. The existence of a document at `/roles_admin/{adminId}` indicates that the user with `adminId` is an administrator. This collection is used exclusively for efficient and atomic security rule checks (using 'exists()') for critical admin permissions, separating rule-based authorization from the 'role' field in UserProfile for clarity and security.

params.name: adminId
params.description: The unique identifier of an administrator, matching their Firebase Authentication UID.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed to be secure, scalable, and easily debuggable, prioritizing Authorization Independence and supporting Query Ability with Security (QAPs).

1.  **Users Collection (`/users/{userId}`)**: This top-level collection stores `UserProfile` documents. The `userId` document ID directly corresponds to the Firebase Authentication UID (`request.auth.uid`). This structure inherently provides path-based ownership for individual users, making it straightforward to write security rules where a user can read or modify only their own profile data (e.g., `match /users/{userId} { allow read, update: if request.auth.uid == userId; }`). For administrative access, the `role` field within the `UserProfile` indicates whether a user is an 'admin' or 'visitor', but for critical security rule checks, a dedicated `roles_admin` collection is used.

2.  **Visits Collection (`/visits/{visitId}`)**: This top-level collection stores `Visit` documents. Each `Visit` document includes a `userId` field, which is a direct reference to the UID of the user who initiated the visit. This is a crucial aspect of **Authorization Independence (CRITICAL)**. Instead of relying on a parent collection or performing a `get()` operation to fetch the user's role or ownership from `/users/{userId}`, the `Visit` document itself contains the necessary `userId` for authorization. Security rules can directly check `request.auth.uid == resource.data.userId` for visitor access. This denormalization eliminates `get()` calls in rules, enhancing atomic operations and simplifying rule logic. Admins (as determined by the `roles_admin` collection) are granted read access to all `visits` to facilitate real-time statistics and reporting.

3.  **Admin Roles Collection (`/roles_admin/{adminId}`)**: This collection serves as a dedicated, existence-based authorization mechanism for administrators. Each document in this collection has an `adminId` that matches the `request.auth.uid` of an administrator. This directly supports the **DBAC (Database Access Control)** principle, where roles are stored in the database. Its primary purpose is to provide a fast and robust check within security rules, like `exists(/databases/$(database)/documents/roles_admin/$(request.auth.uid))`. This check is atomic and doesn't depend on `get()` operations on other documents or complex data. It ensures that only truly authorized administrators can perform sensitive operations, regardless of the `role` field in their `UserProfile` (which could be subject to more lenient update rules or client-side manipulation).

**How the structure achieves Authorization Independence**: Authorization for `UserProfile` is path-based (`/users/{userId}` where `userId` is `request.auth.uid`). Authorization for `Visit` documents leverages denormalization by including `userId` directly in the `Visit` document itself, eliminating the need to `get()` a parent `UserProfile` to determine ownership. Admin authorization is handled via a dedicated `roles_admin` collection, using `exists()` checks, which are atomic and independent of other document data.

**How the structure supports QAPs (Rules are not Filters)**:

*   **`/users/{userId}`**: Individual users can only `get` their own `UserProfile`. For admins, the structure supports querying and listing all users. Admin security rules will explicitly allow `list` and `get` operations on `/users` for users whose `request.auth.uid` exists in the `/roles_admin` collection. This permits the 'User Management' and 'Search Bar' features on the admin dashboard while maintaining security.
*   **`/visits/{visitId}`**: Visitors can create new visits and query (`list`) *only their own* past visits by filtering on `userId == request.auth.uid`. Security rules will enforce `resource.data.userId == request.auth.uid` for visitors' read/update operations. Admins, identified by their presence in `/roles_admin`, are explicitly allowed to `list` all documents in `/visits`. This enables the 'Real-time Statistics Dashboard' and 'Summary Report Generation' features for admins by allowing them to fetch all visit data efficiently, without being constrained by row-level filters that would violate QAP principles for an authorized list operation.
